pattern: Prefix-sum with a frequency map: a subarray sums to k exactly when the difference of two prefix sums equals k, so count earlier prefix sums equal to current minus k in one pass.
hints: A subarray sum equals the difference between two running prefix sums.
If the prefix sum up to j minus k has been seen before at some earlier index, every such earlier index starts a qualifying subarray ending at j.
Maintain a hash map of how many times each prefix sum has occurred, seeded with one occurrence of zero, and add the count of prefix-minus-k as you go.

edge_inputs.description: two distinct subarrays sum to k
negatives and zeros require prefix counting not sliding window
all zeros with k zero counts many subarrays

stress.description: random signed array with a target inside the achievable range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, size))
    arr = [rng.randint(-20, 20) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(-40, 40)
    return [arr, k]
stress.note: small magnitudes keep brute force fast and produce many hits

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def subarraySum(self, nums, k):
        from collections import defaultdict
        seen = defaultdict(int)
        seen[0] = 1
        s = 0
        count = 0
        for x in nums:
            s += x
            count += seen[s - k]
            seen[s] += 1
        return count
